skill.name: browser-task-and-automation-and-delegation
skill.description: Delegates browser automation tasks to a Chrome DevTools specialist agent for navigation, page interaction, form workflows, screenshots, testing, data extraction, and performance analysis.
skill.summary: Route browser automation work to a specialist agent while keeping the main conversation concise.

security_audit.summary: Static external-command, weak-crypto, and entropy findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, task examples, and non-English prose in SKILL.md. The skill still has medium risk because it delegates browser control, external web access, JavaScript data extraction, form submission, screenshots, and file output.

medium_findings.title: Broad Browser Automation Delegation
medium_findings.description: The skill delegates web navigation, UI interaction, form submission, testing, and performance analysis to a Chrome DevTools agent. This is intended behavior, but it can interact with arbitrary external sites and authenticated browser state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly routes browser tasks to a Chrome DevTools specialist agent. The risk is contextual because delegation is the core feature, but the capability can affect real web sessions.

medium_findings.title: JavaScript Data Extraction and Network Monitoring
medium_findings.description: The skill lists JavaScript execution for data extraction and network request monitoring as supported workflows. These operations can expose private page data if used on sensitive sites.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The data extraction and JavaScript behavior is directly documented. No malicious exfiltration endpoint is present, so the finding is medium rather than high.

medium_findings.title: Credential and Authenticated Workflow Handling
medium_findings.description: The examples include filling login forms and navigating to settings after login. The credentials are sample values, but the workflow can handle sensitive account data in real use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The login workflow is shown in clear examples, but the credentials are placeholders. Risk depends on user-provided sites and secrets.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Example URLs
low_findings.description: The skill uses example.com in browser task examples. These are illustrative URLs, not evidence of exfiltration or a command-and-control endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only hardcoded domain observed is example.com, which appears in instructional examples. There is no evidence that the skill sends data to that URL.

low_findings.title: Screenshot File Output
low_findings.description: The skill supports taking screenshots and saving them to files. This can write sensitive page content to local storage if used on private pages.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-saving behavior is directly documented. It is a normal browser automation feature, but it creates a local data exposure risk.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported shell backticks are Markdown formatting and fenced task examples. The reported weak cryptography and entropy alerts are not supported by executable code in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found prose, inline code formatting, and Markdown examples rather than Ruby shell execution or cryptographic code. No executable command path was present in SKILL.md.

dangerous_patterns.title: Delegated Browser Control
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the assistant to delegate browser interaction to a Chrome DevTools agent. Review deployments should ensure this agent follows site, credential, and consent boundaries.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The delegation mechanism is explicit. The danger is capability-based, not evidence of malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Page Script Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes executing JavaScript to extract page data. This can read sensitive page content when run in authenticated browser sessions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The JavaScript extraction capability is documented twice. No hidden or obfuscated script payload was found.

content.value_statement: Browser tasks can flood an AI chat with page snapshots, screenshots, and network details. This skill routes those tasks to a Chrome DevTools specialist agent and returns concise results.
actual_capabilities: Routes browser navigation tasks to a Chrome DevTools specialist agent.
Supports clicking, hovering, dragging, form filling, and form submission workflows.
Captures full-page or element screenshots and page text snapshots.
Runs page performance traces and reports core web metrics.
Extracts page information with JavaScript and monitors network requests.
Tests behavior under simulated network, CPU, and viewport conditions.
limitations: Requires access to a configured chrome-devtools-expert agent.
Does not provide its own browser engine or automation library.
Can expose sensitive page data if used on private sessions.
Needs clear user permission before handling credentials or authenticated pages.

use_cases.title: Verify a Web Flow
use_cases.description: Test a checkout, signup, or settings workflow and receive a concise pass or fail summary.

use_cases.title: Capture Page Evidence
use_cases.description: Open a page, wait for it to load, and collect screenshots or text snapshots for review.

use_cases.title: Analyze Page Performance
use_cases.description: Run a browser trace, inspect key loading metrics, and summarize the main performance bottlenecks.

prompt_templates.title: Open and Summarize a Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Open the provided URL, wait for the page to load, and summarize the visible page content.

prompt_templates.title: Capture a Screenshot
prompt_templates.prompt: Open the provided URL, capture a full-page screenshot, and report where the screenshot was saved.

prompt_templates.title: Test a User Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the requested browser workflow, verify each visible state change, and report any failures with page context.

prompt_templates.title: Profile Loading Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a Chrome DevTools performance trace for the target page and summarize core metrics, bottlenecks, and suggested fixes.

output_examples.input: Open a product page and capture a screenshot.
output_examples.output: The page loaded successfully. A full-page screenshot was saved, and no visible rendering errors were found.

output_examples.input: Test whether adding the first product updates the cart count.
output_examples.output: The product was added successfully. The cart count increased after the button click.
The trace shows slow initial rendering. Large images and render-blocking resources are the main bottlenecks.

best_practices: Provide exact URLs, expected outcomes, and any required test account boundaries.
Use test credentials and non-production accounts for form automation.
Ask for concise summaries unless screenshots, traces, or network details are required.
anti_patterns: Do not run automation on accounts or sites without permission.
Do not include real passwords, payment data, or private tokens in prompts.
Do not request broad scraping when a small targeted extraction is enough.

faq.question: What does this skill automate?
faq.answer: It delegates browser navigation, interaction, screenshots, testing, data extraction, and performance analysis to a Chrome DevTools agent.

faq.question: Does it browse the web directly?
faq.answer: No. It provides routing instructions for a specialist agent that performs browser operations.

faq.question: Can it fill forms?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide form filling and submission when the user provides permission and safe test data.

faq.question: Can it analyze performance?
faq.answer: Yes. It can request Chrome DevTools performance traces and summarize loading metrics and bottlenecks.

faq.question: Is it safe for private pages?
faq.answer: Use caution. Screenshots, page text, network data, and JavaScript extraction can expose private information.

faq.question: Why use delegation?
faq.answer: Delegation keeps large browser outputs out of the main conversation and returns only the important result.
